LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (OF QUALOGY CONSTRUCTION, INC.)THE COURT ADOPTS IT TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. THE SPECIAL DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY IS NOT PROPERLY NOTICED, AND COULD BE OVERRULED ON THIS GROUND. IN ANY EVENT, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN, AND THE DEMURRER ON THIS BASIS IS OVERRULED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT QUALOGY CONSTRUCTION, INC. ("QCI") HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS FOR PIETROFESO TO DETERMINE WHAT ISSUES MUST BE ADMITTED OR DENIED. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIF., INC. (1993) 14 CAL. 4TH 612, 616.) UNCERTAIN. AS TO THE DEMURRERS PURSUANT TO CCP 430.10(E), THESE DEMURRERS ARE OVERRULED BECAUSE QCI HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, COMPARATIVE INDEMNITY, AND DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE FACT THAT PIETROFESO WAS DISMISSED FROM THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT IS NOT DISPOSITIVE OF WHETHER SHE CAN BE A CROSS-DEFENDANT IN A CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY OR COMPARATIVE INDEMNITY. (SEE GEM DEVELOPERS V. HALLCRAFT HOMES OF SAN DIEGO, INC. (1989) 213 CAL. APP. 3D 419, 428; AMERICAN MOTORCYCLE ASSOCIATION V. SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 20 CAL. 3D 578, 584 ("A NAMED DEFENDANT IS AUTHORIZED TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST ANY PERSON, WHETHER ALREADY A PARTY TO THE ACTION OR NOT, FROM WHOM THE NAMED DEFENDANT SEEKS TO OBTAIN TOTAL OR PARTIAL INDEMNITY").) QCI ALLEGES THAT PIETROFESO PERSONALLY DIRECTED QCI TO USE A PARTICULAR SANDBLASTING METHOD TO REMOVE PAINT. (CROSS-COMPLAINT 14, 15, 16.) PIETROFESO'S CONTENTION THAT SHE WAS A CORPORATE OFFICER IS NOT A BASIS TO SUSTAIN THE DEMURRERS. (SEE FRANCES T. V. VILLAGE GREEN OWNERS ASSN. (1986) 42 CAL.3D 490, 504.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (of Qualogy Construction, Inc.)

No appearances. The Court adopts it tentative ruling as follows: Overruled. The special demurrer on the grounds of uncertainty is not properly noticed, and could be overruled on this ground. In any event, the cross-complaint is not uncertain, and the demurrer on this basis is overruled. Cross-complainant Qualogy Construction, Inc. ("QCI") has alleged sufficient facts for Pietrofeso to determine what issues must be admitted or denied. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal. 4th 612, 616.) uncertain. As to the demurrers pursuant to CCP 430.10(e), these demurrers are overruled because QCI has alleged sufficient facts to state a claim for equitable indemnity, comparative indemnity, and declaratory relief. The fact that Pietrofeso was dismissed from the underlying complaint is not dispositive of whether she can be a cross-defendant in a cross-complaint for equitable indemnity or comparative indemnity. (See Gem Developers v. Hallcraft Homes of San Diego, Inc. (1989) 213 Cal. App. 3d 419, 428; American Motorcycle Association v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal. 3d 578, 584 ("a named defendant is authorized to file a cross-complaint against any person, whether already a party to the action or not, from whom the named defendant seeks to obtain total or partial indemnity").) QCI alleges that Pietrofeso personally directed QCI to use a particular sandblasting method to remove paint. (Cross-Complaint 14, 15, 16.) Pietrofeso's contention that she was a corporate officer is not a basis to sustain the demurrers. (See Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490, 504.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE QUALOGY CONSTRUCTION, INC. CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. As to the allegation that cross-defendants insisted on using a less expensive form of sandblasting, the motion is denied; the Court cannot state on this record that this allegation is irrelevant. As to the allegations and prayer for relief regarding attorney fees, the motion to strike is also denied. A prevailing party may potentially be awarded attorneys' fees on an implied indemnity claim under CCP 1021.6. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E.
